issues.title: Permission Guard Pages follow a consistent undocumented pattern
issues.description: A consistent two-layer page pattern exists across admin, payments and studio: a thin `*Page` component handles auth loading and permission check (returning `<AccessDeniedState>` on failure), then delegates to a `*PageContent` component for actual content. This pattern appears in 8+ pages but is not yet documented in architecture rules.
issues.fix: Document this Permission Guard Page pattern in `.claude/rules/architecture.md` under Application-Level Architecture, explaining that `*Page` = permission guard, `*PageContent` = content. This prevents future developers from implementing permission checks inside content components.

issues.title: App-level thin re-export wrappers for shared utilities is an established pattern
issues.description: All apps use one-line re-export wrappers that proxy shared utilities from `shared/*` or `auth/client` into app-local paths. For example, every app has `shared/infrastructure/providers/QueryProvider.tsx` that just does `export { QueryProvider } from "shared/providers"`. This is a deliberate DI/architecture pattern but is not documented.
issues.fix: Document this thin re-export wrapper pattern in `.claude/rules/monorepo-architecture.md` to explain that apps create local wrappers for shared utilities so consumers always import from app-local paths, making it easy to override or extend in the future.

issues.title: Auth feature in each app is a thin re-export — a redundant pattern
issues.description: Every app has `features/auth/application/hooks/useSupabaseAuth.ts` that is a single line: `export { useSupabaseAuth } from "auth/client"`. The features/auth/index.ts barrel then re-exports this. This creates 5 identical files with identical content. The same thin-wrapper approach is already applied at `shared/`, and `features/auth` wrapping seems unnecessary duplication.
issues.fix: Consider merging the `features/auth` wrapper into the `shared/application/` level (which is already the pattern for other auth utilities), or document that `features/auth` is intentionally kept for architectural symmetry even when it's a pure passthrough.

issues.title: URL state pattern (nuqs searchParams) is well-established — but only for product/admin features
issues.description: The nuqs URL state pattern is correctly applied in 5 features (audit, users, products in store/studio, received-orders in payments). However, several paginated or filterable pages in payments (`orders`, `checkout`) and studio (`seller-admins`) do not use searchParams at all — using local `useState` instead, making those pages non-bookmarkable.
issues.fix: Audit `apps/payments/src/features/orders` and `apps/studio/src/features/seller-admins` for filter/pagination state that should use nuqs. The URL state rule already exists in `.claude/rules/url-state.md` — enforcement is the gap.

issues.title: CSS is fully centralized in the shared ui package — all app globals.css are identical
issues.description: All 7 app `globals.css` files are byte-for-byte identical (3 imports + 2 @source directives). This is excellent CSS DRY compliance — all semantic utilities (`surface-grid-dots`, `border-b-strong`, `font-display`, etc.) are defined in `packages/ui/src/styles/utilities.css`. The `css-consistency.md` rule is being followed perfectly.
issues.fix: No fix needed. Consider documenting this as a positive architectural achievement in CLAUDE.md or the css-consistency rule to help future contributors understand this is intentional and complete.

issues.title: eslint-disable i18next comments are widely used for non-string literal suppressions
issues.description: 44 files contain `eslint-disable i18next/no-literal-string` comments. Many are legitimate (HTTP headers, internal permission key strings, API identifiers), but the pattern is common enough that it's worth reviewing. Several suppressions are in places like `domain/constants.ts` and infrastructure files where hardcoded non-UI strings are intentional, suggesting the i18n lint rule may be over-broad for non-presentation layers.
issues.fix: Consider scoping the `i18next/no-literal-string` ESLint rule to `presentation/**/*.tsx` files only, removing the need for suppressions in infrastructure/domain layers. This would reduce noise and make suppressions in presentation files stand out as actual issues.

issues.title: Inconsistent tid() import source across apps
issues.description: Most components import `tid` from `"shared"` directly (the workspace package). However, 4 apps (admin, store, payments, studio) also have a local re-export at `shared/infrastructure/config/tid.ts`. Tests and some components may use either import path, creating inconsistency. The `tid.ts` wrappers just do `export { tid, ... } from "shared/utils/tid"`, so they're redundant but harmless.
issues.fix: Standardize all `tid` imports to `import { tid } from "shared"` (the already-established pattern used in 100+ files). The local `shared/infrastructure/config/tid.ts` wrappers can then be removed as they add no value. Update the e2e-selectors rule to specify the canonical import path.

patterns.description: Permission Guard + Content Page split: `*Page` handles auth/permission checks and delegates rendering to `*PageContent`. Consistent across 8+ pages in admin, payments, and studio. Cleanly separates concerns and enables content components to focus purely on rendering.

Thin re-export wrapper pattern at app-level shared: each app wraps shared package exports via local files (e.g., `shared/infrastructure/providers/QueryProvider.tsx` re-exporting from `shared/providers`). Enables future overrides without touching all consumers.

URL state managed via nuqs searchParams files in domain layer. All filter/pagination hooks use `useQueryStates(featureSearchParams)` with parsers declared in `domain/searchParams.ts`. Consistent in all 5 features that have filters.

All 7 app globals.css files are identical, delegating all CSS to `packages/ui/src/styles/`. Semantic utility classes (surface-grid-dots, border-b-strong, font-display, etc.) are centralized in the ui package and shared across all apps. Perfect CSS consistency compliance.
Barrel index.ts files exist for every feature, enforcing clean public API boundaries. Each feature exports only what's needed via its index.ts.
E2E tests use getByTestId() with tid() as primary selector strategy — no class-based selectors found in spec files. smoke-all-apps.spec.ts tests cross-app session persistence properly.
5 identical `features/auth/application/hooks/useSupabaseAuth.ts` files across apps that each contain a single line re-exporting from `auth/client`. These could be consolidated or removed.
Inconsistent `tid` import source: some files import from `"shared"`, others use a local wrapper at `shared/infrastructure/config/tid.ts`. The canonical import should be `from "shared"`.
